# @cublya/geomap

> Composable React map primitives on d3-geo for SVG maps and globes, with country choropleths, ISO-aware data preparation, markers, great-circle routes, live objects, cameras, static SVG output, and optional UI controls.

@cublya/geomap is an ESM-only React library. It does not bundle basemap data, product data, Tailwind, global CSS, icon dependencies, or fetch logic. Pair it with `@cublya/world-atlas` or another TopoJSON or GeoJSON source, then call `prepareCountries` before rendering country layers.

## Essential Links

- [README](./README.md): install, quickstart, country data, cameras, layers, theming, accessibility, static output, and development commands.
- [Documentation index](./docs/README.md): long-form guides and project documentation.
- [Getting started](./docs/getting-started.md): first map, basemap setup, surface choice, and interaction.
- [API reference](./docs/api-reference.md): public components, hooks, stores, types, utilities, and static rendering APIs.
- [Data and rendering](./docs/data-and-rendering.md): coordinate conventions, country preparation, projections, routes, custom layers, and performance.
- [Theming and accessibility](./docs/theming-and-accessibility.md): presets, CSS variables, controls, tooltip behavior, keyboard support, and reduced motion.
- [Basemap coverage](./docs/basemap-coverage.md): country coverage by `@cublya/world-atlas` resolution.
- [Troubleshooting](./docs/troubleshooting.md): common integration, rendering, camera, styling, and export issues.
- [Examples](./examples/README.md): complete typed example scenarios.

## Public Package Surface

- Main import: `@cublya/geomap`
- Optional stylesheet: `@cublya/geomap/styles.css`
- React surfaces: `GeoMap`, `GeoGlobe`, `GeoView`
- React layers: `CountriesLayer`, `MarkersLayer`, `RoutesLayer`, `LiveLayer`, `GraticuleLayer`
- Optional UI: `GeoControls`, `GeoViewToggle`, `GeoTooltip`
- Camera hooks and stores: `useMapCamera`, `useGlobeCamera`, `useMapView`, `useGlobeView`, `createMapCamera`, `createGlobeCamera`
- Data prep: `prepareCountries`, `lookupIso`, `resolveCountryName`, `ISO_3166_1`
- Geometry and projection helpers: `createFlatProjection`, `createGlobeProjection`, `greatCirclePoints`, `routeLineString`, `geographicBounds`, `toLonLat`
- Static output: `renderStaticMapSvg`, `svgToDataUrl`, `svgToPngBlob`

## Usage Notes For LLMs

- Use React 18 or newer and ESM imports.
- Import country geometry from `@cublya/world-atlas/countries-10m.json`, `countries-50m.json`, or `countries-110m.json` when examples need a basemap.
- Call `prepareCountries(world, { exclude: ["AQ"] })` for country layers in most examples.
- Use `GeoMap` for flat maps, `GeoGlobe` for rotatable orthographic globes, and `GeoView` when an app should switch between both.
- `preset` defaults to `"none"`, which emits no complete visual styling. Use `preset="light"` or `preset="dark"` for ready-to-render visuals.
- No CSS import is required for map rendering. `@cublya/geomap/styles.css` only adds pseudo-class polish for optional HTML helpers such as controls and tooltips.
- Coordinates accept longitude and latitude conventions through helpers, but examples should prefer clear `{ lat, lng }` objects for app data.
- Keep geopolitical changes in the data layer. Use `patchFeatures` in `prepareCountries` when an app needs editorial geometry changes.
- Prefer documented camera methods such as `flyTo`, `fitTo`, `focus`, `zoomIn`, `zoomOut`, and `reset` instead of mutating internal state.
- Static export uses the same data pipeline as `GeoMap`; use concrete colors for export-oriented SVG or PNG examples.

## Development

- Install: `npm install`
- Unit tests: `npm run test`
- Lint: `npm run lint`
- Typecheck: `npm run typecheck`
- Build: `npm run build`
- Full verification: `npm run verify`
- Storybook: `npm run storybook`
- Documentation dev server: `npm run docs:dev`
